assertion_results.text: Output must reference the recent production incident explicitly
assertion_results.evidence: Report header states: 'Context: Post-incident review following payment data corruption event.' Incident Response row reads: 'No prior monitoring; users reported breakage; partial recovery only.'

assertion_results.text: Output must give at least one short-term action item
assertion_results.evidence: The report presents all recommendations as a flat condensed list with no temporal horizon labels (no 'short-term', 'this sprint', 'immediate' categories, or equivalent). Items such as 'Write a regression test that reproduces the race condition first' are urgent in tone but never explicitly designated as short-term action items.

assertion_results.text: Output must recommend monitoring or observability as a priority
assertion_results.evidence: Dedicated 'Monitoring' paragraph: 'Sentry is a start but covers only errors. No latency, throughput, DB performance, or business metrics (payment success rate). Add django-prometheus or Datadog/Grafana Cloud. Add uptime monitoring for critical endpoints.' Monitoring & Observability scores 3/10 in the scorecard.

assertion_results.text: Output must not recommend psychological safety without citing a specific data point
assertion_results.evidence: The report says 'A blameless post-mortem must be completed and shared' and anchors it with the concrete data point 'users detected the outage' under Incident Response. No free-floating psychological safety recommendation appears without supporting evidence.

assertion_results.text: Output must reference the race condition or production data corruption
assertion_results.evidence: Data Integrity section: 'The race condition almost certainly traces to missing SELECT FOR UPDATE or optimistic locking on payment record writes.' Scorecard row: 'Race condition exposed missing DB-level concurrency guards.' Report header also cites 'payment data corruption event'.
